summary: Preliminary C match for the Yoshi's Story stage module grstory. The PR moved most of asm/melee/gr/grstory.s text into src/melee/gr/grstory.c while explicitly leaving .data callback tables in assembly, added grstory.h, wired grstory.c.o into the build, and expanded stage.h with StageInfo bitfields, StageCallbacks, the lbl_803E26F0 callback/string table shape, and a Map struct used by stage ground objects. The body notes the match is preliminary and that some code is awkward, but the newly populated stage data structure members should help later stage decompilation.
what_changed: Added src/melee/gr/grstory.c with 267 lines of C replacing most of grstory text assembly.
Reduced asm/melee/gr/grstory.s by 578 deleted lines while retaining a small assembly/data remainder, consistent with the PR body saying .data callback tables were excluded.
Added src/melee/gr/grstory.h with prototypes for grstory symbols such as func_801E302C, func_801E3030, func_801E30D8, func_801E3418, func_801E366C, lbl_801E36D8, and ITEM_SHYGUY 0xD2.

Expanded src/melee/gr/stage.h: added typedef Vec Vec3, converted StageInfo.unk8C from s8 to an 8-bit bitfield struct, added StageCallbacks, declared the lbl_803E26F0 callback/string table, and introduced a Map struct with x10/x11 flag bitfields and fields through xC8.
Updated Stage_80225074 in stage.c from manual bit shifting on stage_info.unk8C to stage_info.unk8C.b0.
Added grstory.c.o to both e_files.mk and obj_files.mk while keeping grstory.s.o present in obj_files.mk.
Improved function prototypes in include/functions.h for stage-related calls used by grstory: func_801C14D0, func_801C3FA4, func_801C49F8, func_801C7FF8, and func_802D8618.

Implemented grstory behavior including stage initialization, ground object creation through StageCallbacks, GX link setup, Shy Guy item timer/spawn logic using ITEM_SHYGUY, and helper callbacks that still use address-style names.
smart_moves: Left .data callback tables in assembly instead of forcing an incomplete data match; this let the text functions move to C while preserving the remaining unmatched data layout.

Modeled rlwimi-style byte flag access as named bitfields on StageInfo.unk8C and Map.x10_flags/x11_flags, allowing stage code to read/write individual bits directly while matching generated code patterns.
Introduced StageCallbacks and indexed lbl_803E26F0.callbacks[arg0], making the repeated 0x14-byte callback table access in func_801E30D8 understandable and reusable for other stages.
Kept currently unknown callback functions under lbl_801E... names, avoiding premature semantic naming while still exposing accurate prototypes in grstory.h.
Used small inline helpers such as randi(max), which returns 0 for max == 0, and frand_amp1(), which captures repeated random-number idioms visible in the assembly.

Documented suspicious overwritten values in grstory.c comments, such as timer/count values being computed from stage params and then overwritten, preserving evidence without inventing intent beyond possible debugging leftovers.
Populated shared stage structs in stage.h as part of this match, making future ground/stage modules less dependent on raw offsets.
decomp_lessons: For stage modules, partial matches can be valuable even when .data tables remain in assembly; wire the new C object into obj_files.mk/e_files.mk and keep the residual asm object for unmatched data.

When assembly uses byte bit insertion/extraction on stage globals, define bitfield substructures only where observed and immediately update existing users, as with Stage_80225074 using stage_info.unk8C.b0.
Callback tables often become clearer as arrays of small structs; here a 5-function-pointer StageCallbacks layout matched func_801E30D8's callback0/callback2/callback3 usage.

Ground object user_data should be represented by a shared Map struct when multiple stages use common offsets such as map_id at 0x14, callback at 0x1C, flags at 0x10/0x11, and stage-specific fields near 0xC4.
Awkward C can be acceptable for preliminary matching if comments clearly mark known oddities and avoid overclaiming semantics.
Add or refine global prototypes in include/functions.h as C decompilation reaches call sites; stronger prototypes reduce casts and clarify return values like HSD_GObj* from func_801C14D0.
naming_conventions: Stage module files follow gr<stage>.c and gr<stage>.h, here grstory.c/grstory.h.
Unidentified functions and labels retained address-derived names such as func_801E30D8 and lbl_801E31C0.
Struct fields with unknown semantics use offset-style names such as xC4, xC5, xC8, unk0, unk4, and unk8.
Flag bytes are named by offset plus bit names, for example stage_info.unk8C.b0, stage_info.unk8C.b4, stage_info.unk8C.b5, Map.x10_flags.b5, and Map.x11_flags.b012.
Callback table members are mechanically named callback0 through callback4 when exact roles are not established.
The new grstory.h header guard is written as _grtstory_h_, which appears inconsistent with the file name but is the evidence shown in the diff.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted raw assembly calls to func_801C14D0, GObj_SetupGXLink, func_8038FD54, OSReport, func_801C8138, func_801C3FA4, func_801C8858, func_801C2ED0, func_801C7FF8, HSD_Randi, HSD_Randf, func_8026B3C0, and func_802D8618 into typed C call sites.
Matched PowerPC bit operations by assigning bitfield members rather than manually preserving shifts, e.g. stage_info.unk8C.b4 = 0 and b5 = 1.
Preserved a zero-safe random idiom as inline s32 randi(s32 max) { return max ? HSD_Randi(max) : 0; } to match branch behavior around HSD_Randi.
Used temporary unused arrays/variables in functions such as lbl_801E31C0 and func_801E3418 to satisfy stack/layout requirements.
Modeled integer-to-float/random timer behavior in C, including computed assignments that are immediately overwritten by constants like 120, because the original assembly performs both stores.
Kept stage parameter data as an extern anonymous struct pointer lbl_804D69B8 returned by func_801C49F8, with fields unk0, unk4, unk8, and vars[7].
Retained callback/data table symbols such as lbl_803E26F0 in assembly-backed form with only a partial C declaration, avoiding premature full data reconstruction.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that fully match grstory .data callback tables or replace lbl_803E26F0 with a named data structure.
Search for subsequent improvements to StageInfo.unk8C and Map field names after more stage modules were matched.
Check whether grstory.h header guard _grtstory_h_ was later corrected or standardized.
Find later PRs that rename func_801E30D8, func_801E3418, func_801E366C, or the Shy Guy spawn callbacks.
Search other stage matches for reuse of StageCallbacks and Map bitfield layouts introduced here.
